message:
refactor(commands): Improve init, playbook, reflect, and doctor commands

INIT COMMAND (src/commands/init.ts):
- Extract directory paths to variables for cleaner code
- Use expanded paths in success message (shows actual paths, not ~/)
- Paths: reflectionsDir, embeddingsDir, costDir

PLAYBOOK COMMAND (src/commands/playbook.ts):
- Fix variable name: options.category → flags.category
- Reformat addBullet call for better readability

REFLECT COMMAND (src/commands/reflect.ts):
- Pass initialPlaybook as context parameter to curatePlaybook
- Enables curate to use merged playbook for context while mutating
  only the fresh global playbook
- Comment clarifies the two playbook roles:
  freshPlaybook (mutable target) vs initialPlaybook (readonly context)

DOCTOR COMMAND (src/commands/doctor.ts):
- Minor whitespace cleanup

WHY:
These are refinements from code review:
- Init now shows user the actual paths being created
- Playbook uses correct variable name for flags
- Reflect properly separates mutable and readonly playbook references

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-ct1","title":"[LLM] generateContext - Pre-task briefing generation","description":"Function signature:
async function generateContext(
  task: string,
  bullets: string,
  history: string,
  deprecatedPatterns: string,
  config: Config
): Promise<string>

Implementation:
1. Build prompt from PROMPTS.context template
2. Substitute variables:
   - task: what the agent is about to do
   - bullets: relevant playbook rules
   - history: relevant sessions from cass
   - deprecatedPatterns: patterns to avoid
3. Call generateText (not generateObject - free-form output) with:
   - temperature: 0.3
   - maxTokens: 2000
4. Return formatted text briefing

Output format (structured for agent consumption):
- Relevant rules (with IDs for citation)
- Historical solutions (with session paths)
- Warnings about deprecated patterns
- Suggested search queries

Used by: hydrate command before agent starts work.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:46:02.02945-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:11:16.256956-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:11:14.539987-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-cy6","title":"[Utils] jaccardSimilarity - Token-based similarity for deduplication","description":"","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:49.576174-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:49.576174-05:00","comments":[{"id":14,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-cy6","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function jaccardSimilarity(a: string, b: string): number
```

## Implementation Details
- Tokenize both strings using tokenize() helper
- Create sets from token arrays: setA = new Set(tokensA)
- Calculate intersection size: |A ∩ B| = tokens in both sets
- Calculate union size: |A ∪ B| = total unique tokens
- Return |A ∩ B| / |A ∪ B|
- Handle division by zero: if union is empty, return 0

## Why This Matters
Used for semantic deduplication when hash doesn't match but content is similar. Threshold 0.85 recommended for "near duplicate" detection. Catches cases like "Fix bug in parser" vs "Fixed parser bug" which have different hashes but high similarity.

## Edge Cases to Handle
- Empty strings - return 0 (no similarity)
- One empty, one not - return 0
- Identical strings - return 1.0
- No common tokens - return 0
- All tokens common - return 1.0
- Very different lengths - similarity will naturally be lower","created_at":"2025-12-07T23:15:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-d06","title":"[Security] Sanitization config schema","description":"Define the sanitization configuration schema in config.json.

Schema:
```typescript
sanitization: {
  enabled: boolean;           // Global kill switch for sanitization
  extraPatterns: string[];    // Additional regex patterns (user-defined)
}
```

Example config.json:
```json
{
  "sanitization": {
    "enabled": true,
    "extraPatterns": [
      "INTERNAL_[A-Z_]+_KEY",
      "MY_COMPANY_SECRET_\\w+",
      "ACME_CORP_[A-Za-z0-9]{16}"
    ]
  }
}
```

Purpose:
- Allow users to add company-specific or project-specific patterns
- Provide global on/off switch (enabled flag)
- Patterns are strings that will be compiled to RegExp with 'gi' flags

Default config (if not specified):
```json
{
  "sanitization": {
    "enabled": true,
    "extraPatterns": []
  }
}
```

-{"id":"cass_memory_system-d1r","title":"[Diary] listRecentDiaries - Get diaries by age","description":"List diary entries modified within specified number of days.

Function Signature:
async function listRecentDiaries(
  days: number,
  config: Config
): Promise<DiaryEntry[]>

Process:
1. Calculate cutoff date: now - days
2. List all files in diary directory
3. Filter for JSON files matching diary filename pattern
4. Check file modification time (mtime)
5. Keep only files modified after cutoff
6. Load each matching diary using loadDiaryEntry
7. Sort by diary.timestamp descending (newest first)
8. Return sorted array

Sorting:
- Primary: timestamp descending (newest first)
- Secondary: agent alphabetically (for same timestamp)

Error Handling:
- Skip corrupted/unreadable diaries
- Log warnings for skipped files
- Never fail entire operation due to single bad file

Key Features:
- Time-based filtering for recency
- Sorted results for consistency
- Resilient to corrupted entries
- Efficient file stat usage

Use Cases:
- Stats command: 'cass stats --days 7'
- Recent activity summary
- Trend analysis over time windows
- Dashboard recent entries display

Example Usage:
// Get last week's diaries
const recentDiaries = await listRecentDiaries(7, config);
console.log();

// Get today's diaries
const todayDiaries = await listRecentDiaries(1, config);

Performance:
- O(n log n) due to sorting where n = matching diaries
- File stat is fast (no content reading until filter passes)
- Consider pagination for very large results

Output:
Returns array of DiaryEntry objects sorted newest first.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:46:37.928906-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:13:30.163471-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-dke","title":"[Playbook] isDuplicateBullet - Semantic duplicate detection","description":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function isDuplicateBullet(
  newContent: string,
  existingBullets: PlaybookBullet[],
  threshold: number = 0.85
): { isDuplicate: boolean; matchingBullet?: PlaybookBullet }
```

## Purpose
Detect semantic duplicates before adding new bullets. Prevents near-identical rules from cluttering the playbook.

## Algorithm
1. Filter out deprecated bullets (state === "retired")
2. For each active bullet:
   - Calculate jaccardSimilarity(newContent, bullet.content)
   - If similarity >= threshold: return match
3. If no match found: return { isDuplicate: false }

## Jaccard Similarity
```typescript
jaccard(A, B) = |A ∩ B| / |A ∪ B|
```
- Tokenize both strings (split on whitespace/punctuation)
- Calculate intersection size
- Calculate union size
- Return intersection / union

Range: 0.0 (completely different) to 1.0 (identical)

## Threshold Guidelines
- **0.85 (default)**: Very similar, likely duplicate
- **0.75**: Moderately similar, manual review needed
- **0.90**: Almost identical, definitely duplicate

## Example Duplicates (similarity > 0.85)
- "Always run tests before committing"
- "Run all tests before you commit"
→ Same meaning, different wording

## Implementation Notes
- Case-insensitive comparison
- Ignore punctuation differences
- Skip deprecated bullets (may be outdated versions)
- Return first match (don't search all)

## Usage Scenarios
1. **Curator**: Check before adding extracted rule
2. **Manual add**: Warn user if duplicate exists
3. **Merge detection**: Find similar rules to combine

## Dependencies
- jaccardSimilarity() utility function

## Related Functions
- findSimilarBullet() - returns similarity score
- findMergeCandidates() - uses similarity for grouping
- addBullet() - should check duplicates first","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:59.274031-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:55.362698-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-dofz","title":"[Critical] Simplified Diary Schema for V1","description":"# Simplified Diary Schema for V1

## The Problem
Current DiaryEntry schema has 15+ fields including:
- decisions, problems, solutions, tools, patterns
- filesModified, filesByImpact, dependencies
- architectureChanges, codeQuality, testingApproach
- performanceConsiderations, securityNotes, technicalDebt

This is MASSIVELY over-engineered for v1. 
Extracting all this requires multiple LLM calls and complex prompts.
Most fields will be empty most of the time.

## The Solution
Start with a minimal diary schema that covers 80% of value:

### V1 Diary Schema (Minimal):
```typescript
interface DiaryEntryV1 {
  id: string;
  sessionPath: string;
  timestamp: Date;
  
  // Core fields only
  summary: string;         // What happened in 1-2 sentences
  outcome: "success" | "partial" | "failure" | "unknown";
  
  // Optional simple fields
  task?: string;           // What was the user trying to do
  keyInsight?: string;     // One key learning (if any)
  filesChanged?: string[]; // Just the paths
  
  // Metadata
  duration?: number;       // Seconds
  agent: string;           // Which agent
}
```

### Comparison:
| V1 (Minimal) | Current Plan (Full) |
|--------------|---------------------|
| 7 fields | 18+ fields |
| 1 LLM call | 3-5 LLM calls |
| Simple extraction | Complex multi-step |
| Fast (~2s) | Slow (~10s) |
| Works local-only | Requires LLM |

### V1 Extraction (No LLM Required):
```typescript
async function extractDiaryV1(sessionPath: string): Promise<DiaryEntryV1> {
  const session = await loadSession(sessionPath);
  
  return {
    id: generateId(),
    sessionPath,
    timestamp: session.startTime,
    
    // Extract from session metadata (no LLM)
    summary: session.title || "Coding session",
    outcome: inferOutcome(session),  // From exit code, errors
    task: session.userMessages[0]?.slice(0, 200),  // First user message
    filesChanged: extractFilePaths(session),
    duration: session.duration,
    agent: session.agent
  };
}

function inferOutcome(session: Session): Outcome {
  if (session.exitCode === 0 && !session.hasErrors) return "success";
  if (session.exitCode !== 0) return "failure";
  if (session.hasErrors) return "partial";
  return "unknown";
}
```

### Upgrade Path to Full Schema:
```typescript
// V2 adds more fields (with LLM)
interface DiaryEntryV2 extends DiaryEntryV1 {
  decisions?: string[];
  patterns?: string[];
  problems?: string[];
}

// V3 adds rich fields
interface DiaryEntryV3 extends DiaryEntryV2 {
  architectureChanges?: ArchChange[];
  testingApproach?: TestApproach;
  // ... etc
}

// Migration is additive - old entries still valid
```

## Why This Is Critical
- Simpler = faster to implement
- Simpler = less LLM cost
- Simpler = works offline
- Simpler = fewer bugs
- Can always add fields later (additive)
- Full schema is over-engineering for v1","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:07.65785-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:07.65785-05:00"}
